title: Proofpoint TRAP Threat Response Auto-Pull Quarantine Action Audit
dataSources: `sourcetype=proofpoint:quarantine` quarantine, remediation, or TRAP auto-pull actions on messages
app: Proofpoint TAP Modular Input for Splunk (Splunkbase 3681) or Proofpoint On-Demand Add-on with quarantine feed
spl:
index=email sourcetype="proofpoint:quarantine" earliest=-7d
| eval action=coalesce(action, Action, remediation_action, "")
| eval actor=coalesce(admin_user, Admin, operator, system_actor, "trap")
| eval mid=coalesce(message_id, MessageID, msg_id, "")
| where match(lower(action), "(?i)pull|delete|remove|remediate|purge|auto")
| stats count values(action) as actions earliest(_time) as first_seen latest(_time) as last_seen by mid actor
| sort -count
description: Threat Response auto-pull removes malicious mail post-delivery; auditing those actions proves what was retracted, by whom or what automation, and supports investigations when users dispute missing messages.
value: Strengthens accountability for aggressive remediation, supports insider-threat reviews, and satisfies regulators asking for traceability of mailbox interventions.
implementation: (1) Enable quarantine / TRAP audit logging to Splunk. (2) Retain message IDs for correlation to TAP events. (3) Separate human vs system actors. (4) Export weekly digest to IR leadership. (5) Integrate with case management IDs if present.
visualization: Table (message ID, actor, actions, time window), timeline, bar chart (actions per day).
grandmaExplanation: We watch how Proofpoint treats and rewrites mail, so threats and DLP events line up with what the security team already sees in their console.
knownFalsePositives: Quarantine auto-pull during SOC runs; some pulls are part of a playbook, not a mis-fire.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable; primary SPL uses raw or vendor-normalized event fields.
